task_completion.description: Verify that a new folder named 'Champion-Candidate' has been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the folder pane (usually on the left side) of the email client. You should see a folder explicitly named 'Champion-Candidate'.

task_completion.description: Verify that emails have been moved into the 'Champion-Candidate' folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the message list pane while the 'Champion-Candidate' folder is selected or open. There should be emails visible in this folder.

task_completion.description: Verify that there are at least two emails in the folder and they are from the same external sender.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Examine the 'From' or 'Sender' column in the message list of the 'Champion-Candidate' folder. Count the emails: there must be at least 2. Verify that all emails in this folder share the exact same sender email address.

task_completion.description: Verify that a draft email is being composed to the identified sender.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an open email composition window. The 'To:' field must contain the exact email address of the sender whose emails were moved into the 'Champion-Candidate' folder.

task_completion.description: Verify the draft email has an appropriate subject line and a professional invitation in the body.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the email composition window, check the 'Subject:' field for at least one of these keywords: 'Champion', 'Community', 'Invite', or 'Program' (case-insensitive). Also, verify the main text area contains a drafted professional invitation message.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the email client GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show interaction with a standard graphical email client (e.g., Thunderbird), not terminal commands, scripts, or file explorers moving raw Maildir files.

integrity.description: Verify the moved emails are genuine inbox emails and not fabricated by the agent.
integrity.visual_evidence: The emails in the 'Champion-Candidate' folder should have realistic subjects and dates consistent with a pre-existing inbox (SpamAssassin corpus), rather than looking like freshly typed dummy emails.

integrity.description: Verify the invitation is drafted in the email client's compose window.
integrity.visual_evidence: The draft must be visible in the email client's native 'Write' or 'Compose' window, not in a separate text editor like Gedit or Nano.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target folder must be exactly named 'Champion-Candidate'. The selected sender must have at least 2 emails in the folder (valid examples from the dataset include martin@srv0.ems.ed.ac.uk and cout@eircom.net, though others may exist). The outreach email's subject must contain at least one of the following keywords: 'champion', 'community', 'invite', or 'program' (case-insensitive).
